name: USS Ronald Reagan (CVN-76)
detail: Nimitz-class aircraft carrier; 7th Fleet forward-deployed flagship; home ported Yokosuka, Japan. Deployed to Philippine Sea during Aug 2022 PLA exercises. Carrier Air Wing Five (CVW-5) includes F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeyes. Critical deterrent asset for Taiwan contingency planning.

name: F-22A Raptor — Kadena Air Base
detail: USAF F-22A Raptor 5th-generation air superiority fighters rotate through Kadena Air Base, Okinawa, Japan (~650 km from China). Replaced permanently-stationed F-15C/D fleet from 2023. Stealth, supercruise, and advanced avionics make it primary US air superiority asset in Taiwan contingency. Multiple squadrons maintain rotational presence.

name: F-35A/B Lightning II — Japan / Korea
detail: US Marine Corps F-35B and USAF F-35A forward-deployed to Japan and Korea. USMC F-35Bs operate from Iwakuni MCAS; JASDF F-35As increasingly integrated. 5th-gen interoperability with Japan's F-35 fleet critical for combined operations. Short-takeoff/vertical landing F-35B enables operations from dispersed forward sites.

name: B-52 Stratofortress — Andersen AFB, Guam
detail: USAF B-52H Stratofortress long-range bombers on rotational deployment to Andersen AFB, Guam. Strategic deterrence and long-range strike platform; can carry AGM-158 JASSM cruise missiles, Harpoon anti-ship missiles. Routinely patrol near Taiwan Strait signaling US commitment. B-21 Raider eventually to supplement.

name: P-8A Poseidon — Japan, Guam
detail: US Navy P-8A Poseidon maritime patrol and reconnaissance aircraft forward-deployed to Japan (Kadena, Misawa) and Guam. Conducts near-daily flights monitoring PLA naval movements in Taiwan Strait, East China Sea, and South China Sea. Multiple close encounters with PLA interceptors documented. Critical ISR (intelligence, surveillance, reconnaissance) asset for Taiwan contingency.

name: Virginia-Class SSN — Guam / Japan
detail: US Navy Virginia-class nuclear-powered attack submarines forward-deployed at Guam (Naval Base Guam) and periodically Yokosuka. Provide covert ISR, anti-submarine warfare, and Tomahawk land-attack capability against PLA targets. SSNs would be critical early assets in any Taiwan contingency; operate continuously in Western Pacific theater.

name: HIMARS — Taiwan (Delivery in Progress)
detail: High Mobility Artillery Rocket System (HIMARS) approved for sale to Taiwan in the December 2025 $11.154 billion package. Ground-launched precision strike capability with GPS/INS guidance; effective range 70-300 km with extended range munitions. Mobile launchers survivable in dispersed operations; key component of Taiwan's asymmetric 'porcupine' defense strategy. 29 launchers approved.

name: Harpoon Coastal Defense System — Taiwan
detail: Boeing Harpoon Block II+ anti-ship missiles approved for Taiwan ($2.37 billion, 2020); 100 land-based launching systems and 400 missiles. Provides shore-based anti-ship strike out to 120+ km. Designed to target PLA Navy amphibious assault ships in Taiwan Strait. Together with Taiwan's Hsiung Feng III, creates significant coastal defense capability.

name: F-16V Fighting Falcon — ROCAF
detail: Taiwan operates 141 upgraded F-16V (Viper) aircraft — the most capable F-16 variant. Hualien AFB fleet is hardened in underground Chiashan facility for survivability. Armed with AIM-120 AMRAAM, AIM-9X Sidewinder, and Mk-84 precision bombs. Taiwan's primary air combat asset; backed by domestically-produced F-CK-1 Ching-kuo fighters at CCK and Tainan.

name: ROCS Hai Kun (SS-711) — Taiwan's First Indigenous Sub
detail: Taiwan launched its first domestically-built submarine ROCS Hai Kun (SS-711) on September 28, 2023. The diesel-electric submarine represents Taiwan's effort to develop undersea warfare capability after decades of inability to procure modern submarines abroad due to PRC diplomatic pressure. Additional 7 submarines planned under Taiwan's indigenous submarine program. Operational deployment expected 2024-2025.

name: Arleigh Burke-Class DDG — Taiwan Strait Transits
detail: US Navy Arleigh Burke-class guided missile destroyers conduct routine Taiwan Strait transits, typically 1-2 per month as of 2023-2026. Ships carry SM-2/SM-3/SM-6 surface-to-air missiles, Tomahawk cruise missiles, and AN/SPY-1D radar. PLA ships shadow all US transits at close range. Transits reaffirm freedom of navigation and US commitment to open Taiwan Strait.

name: US Army Multi-Domain Task Forces (MDTF) — Pacific
detail: US Army Multi-Domain Task Forces established in the Pacific theater with forward presence in Japan (JGSDF integration) and Philippines (EDCA base access). Integrate cyber, electronic warfare, air and missile defense, and precision strike capabilities. Pacific MDTF specifically designed for Taiwan contingency scenarios requiring long-range fires and multi-domain effects against PLA anti-access/area-denial systems.
